name: Mid-Florida Housing Partnership, Inc.
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit offering free housing counseling, credit guidance, foreclosure prevention, and transitional shelter services across Volusia and Flagler Counties.
description_long:
Mid-Florida Housing Partnership is a HUD-approved nonprofit organization based in Daytona Beach that has served Central Florida residents for over 30 years. The organization was established to expand access to safe, affordable housing through education, counseling, and community-based support across Volusia and Flagler Counties, including communities like Palm Coast, Deltona, DeLand, and surrounding areas.

The organization provides four primary service categories: (1) Homebuyer Assistance with down payment programs and first-time buyer classes, (2) Credit Counseling focused on score improvement and budget building, (3) Foreclosure Counseling for homeowners facing mortgage challenges, and (4) Palmetto House, a transitional shelter specifically for individuals and families experiencing homelessness in Daytona Beach. All services are delivered by trained counselors who provide personalized guidance and step-by-step support.

MFHP distinguishes itself through its deep local roots and comprehensive approach. The organization combines preventative services (homebuyer education, credit counseling) with crisis intervention (foreclosure prevention, emergency shelter). Their team operates directly within Volusia County and demonstrates specific understanding of Central Florida's housing market and community challenges. The organization actively engages the community through workshops, housing fairs, and financial wellness clinics aligned with Fair Housing Month and other community initiatives.

As a free-help nonprofit, MFHP is best suited for individuals and families seeking legitimate, low-cost or no-cost housing guidance without sales pressure or predatory lending. The main caveat is that services are geographically limited to Volusia and Flagler Counties only, and shelter services (Palmetto House) are specifically for homelessness support rather than general housing assistance.
pros: HUD-approved nonprofit status ensures counselors meet federal standards for housing guidance
Over 30 years of operating history demonstrates organizational stability and community trust
Free or low-cost services eliminate financial barriers to accessing housing counseling
Comprehensive service range addresses homebuying, credit improvement, foreclosure prevention, and homelessness
Local team based in Volusia County provides region-specific knowledge of Central Florida housing market
Palmetto House offers transitional shelter specifically for homeless individuals and families
Active community engagement through workshops, fairs, and financial wellness clinics
cons: Services limited geographically to Volusia and Flagler Counties only—not available statewide or nationally
Palmetto House shelter limited to Daytona Beach location with implied capacity constraints
Website does not specify counselor credentials, response times, or wait periods for services
No mention of online counseling options; appears to require in-person visits
Limited detail on down payment assistance program amounts, eligibility criteria, or success rates
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Volusia/Flagler Counties seeking affordable down payment help and education
Individuals facing foreclosure or mortgage delinquency looking for prevention counseling
People with poor credit seeking nonprofit credit counseling and budget-building guidance
Homeless individuals and families in Daytona Beach area needing transitional shelter support
diagnosis: Mid-Florida Housing Partnership is ideal for Volusia and Flagler County residents seeking free, nonprofit housing counseling from HUD-approved advisors. The primary limitation is strict geographic service area—anyone outside these two counties will need alternative resources.
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
